meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: SaaS Starter Kit to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'no portfolio' trap. Use this SaaS starter kit guide with Antigravity and Claude Code to build, validate, and launch your MVP in record time.
content.answer_block: The 'no portfolio' barrier is a mental trap, not a technical one. By deploying a high-velocity SaaS starter kit, you transform abstract ideas into functional, revenue-ready assets. Using Antigravity for architecture and Claude Code for rapid implementation, you can ship a production-grade MVP in days, proving your expertise through execution.
content.intro:
You are staring at a blank repository, the silence of your code editor echoing the lack of a portfolio. Every potential client or investor asks the same fatal question: 'What have you built?' The industry doesn't care about your potential; it cares about your proof. You are stuck in the paradox where you need experience to get work, but you need work to get experience.

It is time to stop waiting for permission to build. A professional SaaS starter kit is your bridge from 'aspirational founder' to 'proven architect.' By leveraging modern AI-assisted engineering, you can compress months of development into a single, high-intensity weekend. We aren't just writing code; we are building a business engine that speaks for itself.

steps.title: Define the Business Value Proposition
steps.description: Before writing a single line of code, map out the core SaaS problem your starter kit will solve. Focus on one high-value feature, like Stripe integration or Auth, to ensure your portfolio piece demonstrates real-world utility.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a CTO. Generate a high-level architecture diagram for a SaaS starter kit that includes user authentication, subscription management via Stripe, and a dashboard for data visualization. Focus on scalability and clean code separation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Create a project roadmap file named ARCHITECTURE.md outlining the core modules and tech stack for a Next.js SaaS starter.'

steps.title: Initialize the Foundation
steps.description: Bootstrap your project with a production-ready stack. We prioritize speed-to-market by using pre-configured boilerplate architectures that handle security and base routing.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a boilerplate directory structure for a Next.js 14 app using TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and Prisma. Include a mock database schema for a multi-tenant SaaS application.

steps.title: Implement Authentication and User Logic
steps.description: Auth is the most critical part of any SaaS. By building this early, you demonstrate that you understand security fundamentals and user session management.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a secure NextAuth configuration for a SaaS app, including Google OAuth and email magic link providers. Ensure the session callback includes the user's subscription tier.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Configure NextAuth.js in app/api/auth/[...nextauth]/route.ts to support multi-tenant session handling.'

steps.title: Integrate Monetization Hooks
steps.description: A SaaS without payments is just a hobby. Integrating Stripe shows you know how to handle real business logic, webhooks, and subscription states.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Stripe webhook handler that listens for checkout.session.completed and updates the user's subscription status in the database.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Add stripe.ts utility and webhook endpoint to handle subscription lifecycle events.'

steps.title: Deploy and Validate
steps.description: Your portfolio is only valuable if it is live. Deploying to Vercel or Railway proves you can manage infrastructure and CI/CD pipelines.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a README.md that explains the technical decisions made in this SaaS starter kit, focusing on why this stack is optimal for rapid scaling.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'git push origin main && vercel --prod'

pro_tips: Always build for multi-tenancy from day one; it differentiates a professional SaaS architecture from a hobby project.
Use a 'feature-first' commit strategy so your Git history serves as a narrative of how you solved specific engineering problems.
Don't build your own UI components; use a library like Radix or Shadcn to show you can integrate professional design systems efficiently.
Include a 'Business Logic' folder in your repo to prove you understand that code is secondary to product functionality.
Document your failures in the README; showing how you refactored a bad decision is more impressive to senior engineers than perfect code.
content.next_steps: Once your starter kit is live, identify one niche problem, build a micro-tool on top of this foundation, and launch it on Product Hunt to validate your new stack.
content.conversion_block: You now have the blueprint to turn 'no portfolio' into a competitive advantage. Stop thinking like a developer and start thinking like a Founder who builds to scale. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
